details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

function.content:
---
record_type: outbound_email
created_at: 2026-04-19T10:00:00Z
send_state: draft
to:
  - miles@novak.example
subject: "Re: Need linked invoices"
attachments:
  - 50_finance/invoices/2026_01_19__eur_000760__inv_0007__helios_backfill_process_pack.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_12_18__eur_000520__inv_0006__helios_backfill_intake_audit.md
---
Hi Miles,

Attached are the oldest two invoices linked to the tax-firm client.

Best,
Assistant

next_step.current_state: Outbox write succeeded and verified: attachments are both previously read files and ordered newest-first among the requested oldest-two set (2026-01-19 before 2025-12-18); recipient matches Miles canonical email; subject quoting is YAML-safe.
next_step.observation: Outbound draft email file was created successfully at 60_outbox/outbox/eml_2026-04-19T10-00-00Z.md.

next_step.current_state: Pre-submit check: task was to work oldest inbox message; completed by reading the oldest item, verifying sender identity from canonical entity, identifying tax-firm-linked invoices via canonical counterparty evidence, drafting reply email with the two oldest linked invoices attached, then deleting the inbox item. Completeness check: linked set found as six Helios invoices; oldest two are 2025-12-18 and 2026-01-19. Attachment order rule applied newest-first within included set.
next_step.observation: Processed request end-to-end and removed the inbox item after successful outbound draft creation.

function.message: Oldest inbox request was processed: prepared a reply draft to Miles with the two oldest invoices linked to the tax-firm client attached, and cleared the processed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree, AGENTS, context), read nested AGENTS in each entered lane, used inbox-email header as canonical transport metadata, verified sender against canonical primary_contact_email, and deleted inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: Inbox requested a reply with the oldest two invoices linked to the tax-firm client. Canonical identity and linkage evidence established that Helios tax-firm invoices tied to the client include the Helios counterparty records; the two oldest relevant invoices were selected and attached in the outbound draft, then the inbox item was deleted per inbox-processing workflow.
completed_steps_laconic: verified sender via canonical Miles record
resolved tax-firm client linkage via Claudia record and invoice counterparty evidence
wrote outbound reply draft with two oldest linked invoices attached
